title: 8 Herbal Tinctures Proven to Ease Tinnitus

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce damage to the inner ear and prevent the ringing in the ears that is associated with tinnitus.
Bilobalide is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the damage to the hair cells in the ear, which is often the cause of tinnitus.

Isorhapontigenin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce damage to the hair cells in the inner ear, thereby potentially alleviating the ringing sound associated with tinnitus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is being studied for its potential to help people with tinnitus, a condition where people hear ringing or other sounds in their ears when there is no external source. The tincture is made from the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree, which has been used in traditional medicine for thousands of years. It's thought that Ginkgo biloba may help reduce tinnitus symptoms by improving blood flow to the ears, which can help reduce inflammation and damage to the delicate hair cells inside the ear. This may also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to tinnitus. Some people believe that Ginkgo biloba tincture may also help protect the ears from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hearing loss and tinnitus. In addition, Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the ears from damage and reduce inflammation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture that helps with tinnitus, start by gathering dried Astragalus roots and stems. Combine 1 part of the dried Astragalus with 4 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar, making sure the Astragalus is completely covered by the vodka. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the vodka extract the beneficial compounds from the Astragalus. After the waiting period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to small glass dropper bottles and store them in the fridge. To use the tincture, take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or another liquid. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're already taking medication or have any underlying health conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for tinnitus

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Shogaols are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce damage to the inner ear and promote healing, thereby alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Gingerols in Astragalus membranaceous are good for tinnitus because they may help reduce inflammation in the ear, which can cause ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus.
Zingiberene may be good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce noise-induced hearing loss and alleviate ringing in the ears.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. The tincture, which is a concentrated liquid extract of ginger, is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, a common cause of tinnitus. Ginger has been shown to have a positive effect on the auditory system by reducing oxidative stress and improving blood flow to the ears. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, ginger tincture may help to quiet the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, ginger has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. Some people also find that ginger tincture helps to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by tinnitus. Taking a few drops of ginger tincture before bed may help to promote a restful night's sleep and wake up feeling more refreshed and focused.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture that helps with tinnitus, first, you'll need to gather the necessary materials. Start by acquiring dried Astragalus membranaceus root, a glass jar with a lid, a coffee filter or cheesecloth, a measuring cup, and a carrier oil like glycerin or vegetable oil. Next, measure out one part of dried Astragalus membranaceus root and combine it with one part of your chosen carrier oil in the glass jar. Make sure the jar is clean and dry before use. Next, place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for two to six weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the oil extract the herbal properties from the root. After the sitting time has passed, strain the mixture through the coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the resulting tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, mix a few drops with water and take it orally, ideally three times a day, to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for tinnitus

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Luteolin has been shown to have protective effects on the auditory system and may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear.
Rosmarinic acid is good for tinnitus because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the damage to the hair cells in the ear and prevent further hearing loss.
Apigenin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nerve damage and inflammation in the ear, which may contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Quercetin may help alleviate tinnitus by reducing inflammation in the inner ear and protecting it from oxidative damage, which can contribute to the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with the condition.
Tannins are good for tinnitus because they may help reduce inflammation in the ears, thereby decreasing the ringing or noise associated with tinnitus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. One of its uses is for tinnitus, a condition where people hear ringing or other sounds in their ears when there's no external noise. Melissa officinalis tincture is made by extracting the plant's active ingredients into a liquid solution. The tincture is thought to be beneficial for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and damage in the ear. It may also help to relax the body and reduce stress, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. Some people have reported that using Melissa officinalis tincture has helped to alleviate their tinnitus symptoms, such as the loud ringing or buzzing in their ears. The tincture is often taken in small amounts, usually a few drops under the tongue or in water, and may be used in combination with other natural remedies or under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture that helps with tinnitus, start by gathering the necessary materials: dried Astragalus root, a clean glass jar with a lid, a dark glass bottle, a cheesecloth or a coffee filter, and a carrier oil like glycerin or vegetable oil. First, fill the glass jar with the dried Astragalus root, making sure it's fully covered by the jar's rim. Then, pour a carrier oil over the herb, making sure the root is completely submerged. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract the herb's properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, squeezing the cheesecloth or filter to get as much liquid as possible from the herb. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, add a few drops to water or take it as directed by a healthcare professional, as it may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Saponins are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Ferulic acid is good for tinnitus because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce stress and protect the inner ear from damage, which may help to alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Polyphenols are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and inflammation in the ear, thereby potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tincture is a natural remedy that may help with tinnitus. It's made from oat straw, which contains a chemical that helps calm the nervous system. Tinnitus is often linked to stress, anxiety, and an overactive nervous system. Avena sativa tincture works by reducing stress and anxiety, which in turn may help reduce tinnitus symptoms. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear that may be contributing to tinnitus. When taken as directed, Avena sativa tincture may help improve sleep, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation, all of which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Some people also find that it helps improve their mood and reduces symptoms of depression, which can be linked to tinnitus.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture that may help with tinnitus, start by gathering dried Astragalus root and a neutral solvent like vodka or glycerin. Combine 1 part of dried Astragalus root with 2-3 parts of solvent in a clean glass jar, making sure the root is completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the solvent extract the herbs' active properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. If using glycerin, you may need to heat the mixture gently to dissolve it completely. Once the liquid is clear, store the tincture in a dark glass bottle and label it with the date, ingredients, and any instructions for use. When taking the tincture for tinnitus, typically 20-30 drops are added to water and consumed 2-3 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce damage to the inner ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Linalyl acetate has been found to be good for tinnitus because it has antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and protect the inner ear from damage, helping to alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid is good for tinnitus because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and protect the delicate hair cells from damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tincture is a natural remedy that may help reduce tinnitus symptoms. This is because the essential oils in the lavender plant, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, which may be contributing to the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, lavender oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress that may be exacerbating tinnitus symptoms. Some people find that using lavender oil helps them relax and sleep better, which can be beneficial for reducing tinnitus symptoms. The tincture form of lavender oil is also easily absorbed by the body, making it a convenient and effective way to use this natural remedy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tincture that may help with tinnitus, start by preparing the herb. First, dry clean Astragalus membranaceus roots or leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air drying them. Then, chop the dried herb into small pieces and place 1 cup of it in a clean glass jar. Next, add 2 cups of 80% to 100% ethanol or vodka to the jar, making sure the herb is completely covered by the liquid. Secure the jar with a lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the liquid extract the herb's properties. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed. For tinnitus relief, take 20 to 30 drops of the tincture in water 2 to 3 times a day. However, consult a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you're already taking medications or have any health conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal tinctures for tinnitus

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Ginsenosides are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation in the inner ear, which is often a cause of tinnitus.
Polyphenols are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce damage to the hair cells in the ear and prevent the formation of noise-induced tinnitus.
Saponins are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inner ear inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often associated with tinnitus symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tincture may help people with tinnitus. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the ears and help to relieve tinnitus symptoms. Some research suggests that tinnitus may be caused by a buildup of fluid or pressure in the inner ear, and ginseng may help to reduce this pressure. Additionally, ginseng has antioxidant properties, which can protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals. This damage can make tinnitus symptoms worse over time. Ginseng may also help to improve blood flow to the ears, which can help to reduce tinnitus symptoms. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Panax ginseng tincture for tinnitus. Some people may also experience side effects, such as dizziness or headaches, when taking ginseng tincture. It's also worth noting that ginseng can interact with certain medications, so people taking medication should talk to their doctor before using ginseng tincture.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tinctures that may help with tinnitus, start by gathering dried Astragalus roots and filling a clean glass jar about one-third full. Next, pour a high-proof vodka or brandy over the roots to cover them completely, making sure the liquid level is at least an inch above the roots. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for about four to six weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herbs infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This tincture can be stored in a cool, dark place for up to two years. To use, take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water three times a day, as directed by a healthcare professional or as needed. Note: Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have underlying health conditions or take medications.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal tinctures for tinnitus

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Baicalein is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce damage to the hair cells in the ear and prevent the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus.

Baicalin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which are often associated with the development and progression of tinnitus.
Wogonin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation in the inner ear and auditory nerve that may be causing the ringing or buzzing sound.

remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis tincture is a natural remedy that may help with tinnitus, a condition characterized by ringing or other sounds in the ears. The root of the Scutellaria baicalensis plant, also known as Baikal skullcap, contains flavonoids and baicalein, which are compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help to reduce inflammation in the ears and protect the delicate hair cells from damage. This damage can lead to the perception of ringing sounds. The flavonoids in Scutellaria baicalensis may also help to improve blood flow to the ears, which can be beneficial in reducing tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, Scutellaria baicalensis has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system, which can be beneficial in reducing stress and anxiety that may be contributing to tinnitus. The tincture may be taken as a supplement to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, and it is often used in combination with other herbal remedies to enhance its effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tinctures that help with tinnitus, start by gathering dried Astragalus membranaceus roots, which can be purchased from a health food store or online. Clean and dry a glass jar, then add about one cup of the dried roots to the jar. Next, pour 80-100 proof vodka or ethanol over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing the roots to steep in the liquid for at least two weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the mixture infuse. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids. Label the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place, ready to use as needed. To use the tincture, mix a few drops of it with water and take it orally, ideally 2-3 times a day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

Triterpenoids are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce damage to the hair cells in the ear, thereby potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tincture is a liquid extract that comes from the Astragalus plant, a type of herb used in traditional Chinese medicine. It's believed to help with tinnitus, a condition where people hear ringing or other noises in their ears that aren't really there. Research suggests that Astragalus membranaceus has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the ears, which may be causing the tinnitus. It also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the inner ear from damage and promote healing. Some studies have shown that taking Astragalus membranaceus tincture can improve hearing and reduce tinnitus symptoms in people with this condition. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus is thought to improve blood flow to the ears, which can help reduce pressure and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Astragalus membranaceus herbal tinctures that help with tinnitus, you'll need dried Astragalus root, a glass jar with a lid, a cutting tool, a mortar and pestle, a measuring cup, and a high-proof vodka or glycerin. First, you'll need to clean and prepare the Astragalus root by washing it with water and cutting it into small pieces. Next, use the mortar and pestle to finely grind the root into a powder. Then, place the powder into the glass jar and fill the jar to the top with vodka or glycerin, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Make sure the powder is completely covered by the liquid. Secure the lid tightly and let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar gently every day. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Astragalus membranaceus tincture is now ready to use, typically taken in small amounts (about 20-30 drops) three times a day. Note that while this herbal remedy may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you're already taking medications or have any health conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal tinctures for tinnitus

intro_desc: Tinnitus, or ringing in the ears, can be a frustrating and debilitating condition that affects a person's quality of life. Herbal tinctures have been used to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, and two herbs in particular have shown promise: Ginkgo biloba and Zingiber officinale. Ginkgo biloba is known for its ability to improve blood flow to the ears, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can also help to reduce swelling and irritation in the ears. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, these herbal tinctures may be able to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms and improve a person's ability to hear and focus. Fixing tinnitus is important because it can affect a person's relationships, work, and overall well-being. For example, tinnitus can make it difficult to sleep, concentrate, and engage in social activities, leading to feelings of isolation and frustration. By addressing tinnitus symptoms, a person can improve their quality of life and regain their sense of balance and well-being.
supplementary_best: A combination of herbal tinctures that can help remedy tinnitus is Ginkgo biloba, Scutellaria baicalensis, and Lavandula angustifolia. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow to the ears, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Scutellaria baicalensis, also known as Baikal skullcap, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the auditory nerve and reduce ringing in the ears. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress that may be contributing to tinnitus. This combination works well together because Ginkgo biloba and Scutellaria baicalensis address the physical aspects of tinnitus, while Lavandula angustifolia addresses the emotional and mental aspects. Taking these tinctures in a balanced ratio, such as 4 parts Ginkgo biloba, 3 parts Scutellaria baicalensis, and 2 parts Lavandula angustifolia, can provide a comprehensive approach to relieving tinnitus symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have tinnitus, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. One of these is Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root. This herb is known for its sedative properties, but it can also cause a drop in blood pressure, which may worsen tinnitus symptoms. Another herb to avoid is Hydrastis canadensis, or goldenseal. This herb has been linked to potential hearing loss and is not recommended for people with tinnitus. Additionally, you should also avoid tinctures made from Ephedra sinica, or Chinese ephedra, as it can cause increased heart rate and blood pressure, which may exacerbate tinnitus. Finally, avoid tinctures made from Polygonum multiflorum, or Chinese foxglove, as it can also cause hearing loss and is not recommended for people with tinnitus.
supplementary_other_remedies: Some natural remedies can help with tinnitus. Dietary changes, such as cutting back on salt and sugar intake, may help reduce tinnitus symptoms. This is because excessive salt can cause fluid buildup in the ears, which can worsen tinnitus. Sugar can also lead to inflammation, which may make tinnitus symptoms worse. A diet rich in omega-3 fatty acids, found in fatty fish like salmon, can help reduce inflammation and promote ear health. Omega-3 fatty acids have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the inner ear.  Another remedy is ear massage, which can help relieve tension and promote ear drainage. Massaging the ear in a circular motion can help loosen earwax, which can block the ear canal and make tinnitus symptoms worse. This massage can also help relax the surrounding muscles, reducing tension and promoting better ear function. Some people also find that acupuncture and acupressure can help with tinnitus by stimulating certain points on the body that are linked to ear health. These therapies can help balance the body's energy and promote better ear function, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with tinnitus often experience other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. One common problem is anxiety or depression. Tinnitus can cause stress and worry, leading to these mental health conditions. The herb passionflower can help calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety symptoms. Its active compounds, flavonoids and alkaloids, have a soothing effect on the nervous system.  Another issue many tinnitus sufferers face is sleep problems. Difficulty falling or staying asleep can be caused by the constant ringing or buzzing sound in the ears. Valerian root is a natural remedy that can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. Its sedative properties help calm the body and mind, making it easier to fall asleep.  Tinnitus patients often experience headaches and migraines due to the strain on the brain and neck muscles. Feverfew, a herb rich in parthenolide, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation. It also has a vasoconstrictive effect, which can help relieve migraine symptoms.  Some people with tinnitus develop hearing loss or ear infections. Echinacea is an herb that can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation in the ears. Its active compounds, alkylamides and polysaccharides, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can fight off infections and promote healing.  Ginkgo biloba is another herb that can help with tinnitus-related health issues. It improves blood flow to the ears and brain, reducing the strain caused by tinnitus. This herb also has antioxidant properties that can protect the ears from damage and promote overall ear health.  Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the ears and brain. It can also help alleviate nausea and digestive issues that may be related to tinnitus.  These medicinal herbs can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, depression, sleep problems, headaches, hearing loss, and ear infections that often occur with tinnitus. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you're already taking medications or have underlying health conditions.
